source_plan_ref: operator working note outside the repository: aoa-evals-strategic-understanding-notes.md

requirements_review.claim_limit: The repo now routes proof as a positive owner operation with explicit stop-lines. This does not make negative boundary language the architecture and does not complete the long goal.
Durable re-entry, steering, working artifacts, and proof-loop review are now routeable from files. This does not create hidden schedules, hidden verdicts, or autonomous runtime authority.
Sibling and center lessons are adapted as proof-boundary routes. This does not absorb sibling owner truth or copy another repo literally.
Authority classes, sibling refs, and source-derived-candidate-receipt splits are mapped before further movement. This does not make the map stronger than the source surfaces.
The root design trio is present and routeable. It does not replace bundle-local proof meaning, architecture depth, or eval philosophy.
Durable decisions now explain topology, validation, route, and report choices. They remain rationale, not proof source authority.
Direction, release narrative, obligation records, quest lifecycle, and generated readers are separated. Quests still do not become eval bundles or verdict authority.
The convex topology criterion is now carried by explicit source, derived, candidate, receipt, sibling, legacy, and mechanic classes. It does not authorize arbitrary file movement.
Mechanics packages route real proof operations and name stronger owner splits. They do not move source bundles or become empty taxonomy.
Legacy and accepted-input names are preserved as provenance and routing compatibility. They do not steer active topology by historical inertia.
Validator and tests now constrain named design, topology, quest, mechanics, report, receipt, release, and closeout invariants. Green checks do not prove broader truth outside those invariants.
The active proof loop is locally usable through smoke, bundle-local report, report index, and dry receipt intake. It still publishes no live receipt and promotes no bundle.
Runtime and machine evidence are candidate intake surfaces for bundle-local review. This audit does not mutate abyss-stack, run live machine integration, or accept runtime verdict authority.
The maintained Spark lane is under .agents and route-card covered. It does not widen proof claims or make .agents a doctrine center.
The local release-prep handoff is reviewable after broad local gates. It is not a tag, GitHub Release, PR approval, GitHub Repo Validation, or goal completion.
Known traps are audited into route, validation, and open-landing boundaries. This report is a handoff audit, not a completion ceremony.

trap_review.mitigation: The working note was turned into repo-local design, decision, mechanics, report, quest, and validator surfaces rather than left as private memory.
Root design routes to bundle-local source truth and depth docs instead of replacing them.
Decision notes are tied to concrete topology, validation, mechanics, report, and route changes.
Questbook, quest source records, lifecycle states, generated readers, roadmap, and eval verdict meaning are separated.
Mechanic packages exist only for named proof operations with validators and stronger-owner splits.
Sibling refs are local compatibility evidence with canary coverage, not cross-repo mutation or authority transfer.
Runtime evidence is candidate-only and does not pull abyss-stack into aoa-evals as a live owner.
Route docs now name owned operations, inputs, outputs, and handoffs before stop-lines.
Legacy names are mapped as provenance, accepted inputs, generated projections, or retire-after vocabulary.
Validator and tests check semantic statuses, open publication boundaries, required commands, and report subordination.
Release readiness is separated from actual tag, PR approval, GitHub Repo Validation, and goal completion.
The first proof-loop path ends in bundle-local report and dry receipt intake only, with no live publication.

verification_snapshot.claim_limit: Focused strategic closeout tests constrained this handoff report after it was added.
Validates repository invariants for the current local checkout; it does not prove GitHub Repo Validation for an unmerged PR.
Checks shaped semantic AGENTS docs; it does not prove every future agent route is complete.
Checks nested route-card coverage; it does not prove source proof meaning.
Whitespace diff hygiene passed; it does not prove semantic completion.
Checks generated catalog freshness; generated output remains subordinate to source bundles.
Checks report index freshness; the report index is not receipt or verdict authority.
Checks runtime candidate template index freshness; runtime candidates remain candidate evidence.
Checks runtime candidate intake freshness; intake does not accept runtime verdict authority.
Checks phase-alpha matrix freshness; phase-alpha naming remains legacy or projection posture where applicable.
Latest-sibling canary is local compatibility evidence; it is not sibling release approval.
Full local tests passed for this checkout; tests do not prove the strategic goal is complete.
Local release audit passed; it is not a tag, GitHub Release, PR approval, or proof that eval claims grew stronger.

open_items_before_goal_completion: review the accumulated diff as one bounded strategic refactor scope
commit the intended diff on a branch based on current origin/main
open a PR that names changed surfaces, validation, skipped checks, and remaining risk
observe GitHub Repo Validation for the PR
merge only after required checks are green
return to main, fast-forward from origin/main, and confirm a clean worktree
create any tag or GitHub Release only after the release-prep change lands on main
publish a live eval-result receipt only through a separate receipt route, if the owner chooses to do so
run a final owner-visible goal completion audit after landing, not before
claim_limit: This strategic closeout audit states local strategic refactor handoff readiness with open landing. It does not mark the goal complete, does not open a PR, does not observe GitHub Repo Validation, does not publish a release, does not create a tag, does not publish a GitHub Release, does not publish an eval result receipt, does not promote any bundle, does not accept runtime evidence, and does not mutate sibling repos.
